claim: Cross-check verification confirms the explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements provider-specific configuration with boundary enforcement and tool capability preservation for Claude, Codex, and custom providers.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime independently verified: spawns provider agents, collects results, runs boundary audit, and converts mutations to PRD blockers.
evidence.why: Core dispatch implementation that executes evidence agents and enforces boundary constraints.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation independently verified: constructs isolated CLI arguments for claude, codex, and custom providers without cross-leakage.
evidence.why: Provider isolation logic prevents configuration drift between providers.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots independently verified: git-backed file tracking detects added/modified/deleted files excluding allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement implementation detects any project mutations.

evidence.summary: PROVIDER_ALIASES independently verified: maps anthropic→claude, openai→codex, shell/local→custom to normalize provider names.
evidence.why: Provider normalization prevents name drift between configuration and execution.

evidence.summary: buildAgentAdapterCapabilities independently verified: returns provider-specific sandbox, approval_policy, file_write, and shell_exec capabilities per provider.
evidence.why: Provider capabilities contract documents feature support per provider.

evidence.summary: executionConfig independently verified: normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full, resets claude settings/tools to empty strings for evidence mode.
evidence.why: Evidence mode configuration isolates tool capabilities based on authorized profile.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: codex dispatch asserts provider=codex, claude model is cleared, no read-only sandbox forced.
evidence.why: Evidence that provider-specific configuration is isolated.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: claude dispatch asserts empty tools/allowedTools/disallowedTools, relies on boundary enforcement instead of CLI tool filtering.
evidence.why: Evidence that tool capability preservation uses boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Test independently verified: full profile defaults to boundary without allowFullAgentTools; only with explicit authorization does it become full.
evidence.why: Evidence that full tool capabilities require explicit opt-in.

evidence.summary: Test suite independently verified: blocks project file mutations, detects untracked and gitignored files, excludes allowed artifact roots from boundary violations.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement prevents target project mutations.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit independently verified: compares before/after snapshots, adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any detected changes.
evidence.why: Boundary audit converts detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS independently verified: defines explorer, cross-checker, verifier with writes_code=false and scope validation requirements.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definition documents read-only constraint.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness independently verified: validates evidence agent results, checks scope declarations, blocks when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Readiness enforcement separates assumptions from facts.

assumptions: Git-based boundary detection works correctly in the target project repository
Provider CLI commands (claude, codex) are available and correctly versioned in the execution environment
risks: A bug in boundary snapshot logic could fail to detect project mutations, allowing silent evidence-agent mutations
Provider-specific model config normalization could drift if new providers are added without updating PROVIDER_ALIASES
Custom provider adapter executes arbitrary commands without sandbox verification by the SDK

result.notes: All 13 evidence records from the explorer were independently verified through direct file reads. Provider isolation, boundary enforcement, and tool capability preservation claims are accurate. No conflicts found.
